The methodology to compute Quantile Epidemic State (QUEST) thresholds
was developed in 2025 at Sciensano, the Belgian Institute for Health.
While other methods for thresholds of respiratory surveillance signals
aim to classify seasonal intensity, allowing for comparison between
seasons, the QUEST thresholds were developed to do week-to-week
surveillance of the epidemiogical situation. A central objective during
development was to ensure these thresholds were easy to interpret and
directly relevant to monitoring the burden of respiratory pathogens on
the healthcare system. In short, the method uses a hidden Markov model
to define the epidemic state and defines thresholds as specific
quantiles of observed epidemic state incidences.

# Package index

## All functions

- [`create_decoding_plots()`](create_decoding_plots.md) : Visualize
  decoded hidden states
- [`create_hmm_plots()`](create_hmm_plots.md) : Visualize hidden Markov
  model results
- [`create_loop_plots()`](create_loop_plots.md) : Visualize the
  stability of HMM states and thresholds
- [`create_threshold_plots()`](create_threshold_plots.md) : Visualize
  hidden Markov model and threshold results
- [`df_gp_be`](df_gp_be.md) : Belgian GP workload data
- [`df_sari_be`](df_sari_be.md) : Belgian SARI data
- [`run_hmm()`](run_hmm.md) : Fit a hidden Markov model (HMM) to
  epidemiological time series data
- [`run_loop_thresholds()`](run_loop_thresholds.md) : Stability analysis
  of QUEST thresholds via iterative refitting
- [`run_out_of_sample_decoding()`](run_out_of_sample_decoding.md) :
  Out-of-sample state decoding using a frozen HMM
- [`run_threshold_computation()`](run_threshold_computation.md) :
  Compute QUEST thresholds
- [`summary(`*`<epiquest_hmm>`*`)`](summary.epiquest_hmm.md) : Summary
  of hidden Markov model fit
- [`summary(`*`<epiquest_loop>`*`)`](summary.epiquest_loop.md) : Summary
  of HMM stability analysis
- [`summary(`*`<epiquest_thresholds>`*`)`](summary.epiquest_thresholds.md)
  : Summary of QUEST thresholds
